protocol_definition.name: Shared Blackboard
protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents post structured contributions to a shared blackboard; Writer synthesizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Every agent sees the task and current full blackboard.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after all rounds or budget exhaustion, then Writer output.

# AI Meeting-Assistant Tools: Market Comparison for a Small University Research Lab

## 1. Brief Market Context

The AI meeting-assistant market has matured rapidly, with tools now offering real-time transcription, speaker identification, automated action-item extraction, and searchable archives. For a 12-member university research lab using Zoom and Google Meet, the key considerations are budget (academic/nonprofit pricing), platform compatibility, privacy/consent compliance (especially for research conversations involving human subjects or sensitive data), and data-retention policies. The market divides into **platform-integrated** tools (bundled with Zoom or Google Workspace) and **standalone** tools (third-party bots that join meetings). Below is a comparison of four currently available options.

## 2. Comparison Matrix

| Product | Standalone or Integrated | Supported Meeting Platforms | Key Features | Public Pricing / Plan Notes | Privacy or Data-Retention Notes | Best Fit | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Zoom AI Companion** | Integrated (with Zoom paid accounts) | Zoom only | Meeting summaries, action-item extraction, smart recording, speaker identification, searchable transcripts, whiteboard summarization | Included at no extra cost for paid Zoom accounts (Pro, Business, Enterprise). Pro: ~$15.99/host/month. No separate AI Companion fee. | Zoom’s privacy policy states AI Companion does not use customer audio/video/chat for training its AI models. Data retention follows Zoom’s standard policies; transcripts stored in Zoom Cloud. Consent: host controls recording notification. | Labs already using Zoom paid accounts; no additional cost. | Only works within Zoom; no Google Meet support. Action-item extraction is less mature than standalone tools. |
| **Google Gemini for Workspace (AI Meeting Notes)** | Integrated (with Google Workspace) | Google Meet only | AI-generated meeting summaries, auto-captions, speaker identification, searchable transcripts in Google Drive, action-item detection | Requires Google Workspace subscription + Gemini add-on. Workspace Business Starter: ~$6/user/month; Gemini add-on: ~$20/user/month (or included in Gemini Enterprise). Academic pricing may be available but not publicly detailed. | Google’s privacy policy states customer content is not used for model training without consent. Data stored in Google Cloud; retention follows Workspace admin policies. Consent: meeting recording/captions notification by host. | Labs already using Google Workspace for Education; Gemini add-on may be discounted. | Only works within Google Meet; high per-user cost for Gemini add-on. Action-item extraction is basic. |
| **Otter.ai** | Standalone | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex | Real-time transcription, speaker identification, searchable transcripts, automated action-item extraction, export to PDF/CSV/TXT, Otter Chat for Q&A | Pro: ~$16.99/user/month (billed annually). Business: ~$30/user/month (billed annually). Free tier available (limited 300 min/month). No specific academic/nonprofit discount publicly listed. | Otter’s privacy policy states it may use de-identified data to improve services. Users can request deletion. Transcripts stored on Otter servers; retention period not explicitly stated. Consent: bot joins meeting; host notification required. | Labs needing cross-platform support and robust action-item extraction. | Per-user cost adds up for 12 members. Data stored on third-party servers. Privacy policy less restrictive than integrated options. |
| **Fireflies.ai** | Standalone | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex, RingCentral | Automatic transcription, speaker identification, searchable transcripts, action-item extraction, topic tracking, sentiment analysis, export to 40+ integrations (Notion, Asana, Slack) | Pro: ~$18/user/month (billed annually). Business: ~$29/user/month (billed annually). Free tier available (limited 5 credits/user). No specific academic/nonprofit discount publicly listed. | Fireflies’ privacy policy states it does not sell user data. Transcripts encrypted at rest and in transit. Data retention: users can delete transcripts; default retention not explicitly stated. SOC 2 Type II compliant. Consent: bot joins meeting; host notification required. | Labs needing rich integrations and advanced analytics. | Per-user cost is high for 12 members. Third-party data storage. Sentiment analysis may be unnecessary for research meetings. |

## 3. Market Trends and Differentiators

- **Platform-integrated vs. standalone tradeoff:** Integrated tools (Zoom AI Companion, Google Gemini) offer zero additional cost if the lab already has paid accounts, and they keep data within the existing platform ecosystem—reducing third-party privacy risk. However, they lock the lab into a single platform and often have less sophisticated action-item extraction and search capabilities compared to standalone tools.
- **Privacy as a differentiator:** Zoom AI Companion’s explicit policy of not using customer audio/video/chat for AI training is a strong selling point for research labs. Standalone tools like Otter and Fireflies have more complex privacy policies that may allow de-identified data use for model improvement.
- **Academic pricing gap:** None of the standalone tools publicly advertise academic or nonprofit discounts, though some may offer them upon request. This is a notable gap for budget-sensitive labs.
- **Export and interoperability:** Standalone tools generally offer richer export options (PDF, CSV, integrations with project management tools) than integrated tools, which tend to keep data within their own ecosystem.

## 4. Recommendation for the 12-Member Lab

**Primary recommendation: Zoom AI Companion (if the lab already uses or can adopt Zoom paid accounts).**

- **Rationale:** Zero additional cost for existing Zoom subscribers; strong privacy protections (no AI training on customer data); speaker identification and searchable transcripts are included; data stays within Zoom’s infrastructure, reducing third-party risk.
- **Limitation:** Does not support Google Meet. If the lab uses both Zoom and Google Meet equally, this is a significant gap.

**Secondary recommendation (hybrid approach): Use Zoom AI Companion for Zoom meetings + Otter.ai free tier (or Pro for one shared account) for Google Meet meetings.**

- **Rationale:** Covers both platforms. Otter’s free tier provides 300 minutes/month of transcription, which may suffice for occasional Google Meet calls. A single Pro account (~$16.99/month) could be used for higher-volume Google Meet transcription, with notes shared via export.
- **Tradeoff:** Managing two tools adds complexity. Privacy policies differ between platforms.

**If the lab primarily uses Google Meet and has Workspace for Education:** Investigate whether Google Gemini for Workspace is available at a discounted academic rate. If the Gemini add-on cost is prohibitive, Otter.ai or Fireflies.ai are viable standalone alternatives.

## 5. Risks, Assumptions, and Verification Notes

- **Pricing accuracy:** Pricing listed is based on publicly available information as of early 2025. Actual costs may vary based on region, promotions, or negotiated academic discounts. Verify directly with vendors.
- **Academic discounts:** None of the standalone tools publicly list academic pricing. Labs should contact sales teams to inquire about nonprofit or educational discounts.
- **Privacy and consent:** All tools require the meeting host to enable recording/transcription and notify participants. Labs conducting research with human subjects must ensure compliance with their IRB protocols regarding recording and third-party data processing.
- **Data retention:** Otter.ai and Fireflies.ai do not publicly specify default data-retention periods. Labs should review each tool’s Data Processing Agreement (DPA) and configure auto-deletion policies where available.
- **Feature maturity:** Action-item extraction quality varies. Zoom AI Companion’s action-item detection is newer and less refined than Otter’s or Fireflies’. Labs should test with sample meetings before committing.
- **Platform lock-in:** Integrated tools tie the lab to a single platform. If the lab switches video-conferencing providers, integrated features are lost.
- **Verification:** All claims above were sourced from official product pages, pricing pages, and privacy policies accessed via web search. Specific URLs are listed in the source list below.

## 6. Source List

1. **Zoom AI Companion features and pricing** – Zoom Support. "AI Companion." https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/16147340559117-AI-Companion (accessed March 2025).
2. **Zoom pricing plans** – Zoom. "Pricing." https://zoom.us/pricing (accessed March 2025).
3. **Google Gemini for Workspace pricing** – Google Workspace. "Pricing." https://workspace.google.com/pricing (accessed March 2025).
4. **Google Gemini for Workspace meeting notes** – Google Workspace. "Gemini for Google Workspace." https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/ (accessed March 2025).
5. **Otter.ai pricing plans** – Otter.ai. "Pricing." https://otter.ai/pricing (accessed March 2025).
6. **Otter.ai privacy policy** – Otter.ai. "Privacy Policy." https://otter.ai/privacy (accessed March 2025).
7. **Fireflies.ai pricing plans** – Fireflies.ai. "Pricing." https://fireflies.ai/pricing (accessed March 2025).
8. **Fireflies.ai privacy policy** – Fireflies.ai. "Privacy Policy." https://fireflies.ai/privacy (accessed March 2025).
9. **Fireflies.ai security and compliance** – Fireflies.ai. "Security." https://fireflies.ai/security (accessed March 2025).
